nation: Mississaugas of the Credit First Nation
one_time_description: Toronto Purchase Trust revenue of $11,590,680 from land claim settlement trust income (significantly higher than prior year $6,681,893)
economic_notes: First Nation owns 100% of 8392692 Canada Inc., MCBC, and Nanticoke Corporation (GBEs accounted for by modified equity method). Also owns 99.9% of Mississaugas of the Credit Business LP. New Credit Variety and Gas Bar is fully consolidated. Toronto Purchase Trust holds $144M+ settlement from 2010 Toronto Purchase Specific Claim. Nanticoke Solar LP interest exchanged for shares in Nanticoke Corporation valued at $1,464,647.
notes: Amended and restated financial statements (restated from original October 5, 2021 report, see Note 28). Fiscal year is 2021 not 2024. Province is Ontario. Three GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: 8392692 Canada Inc., MCBC, and Nanticoke Corporation. New Credit Variety and Gas Bar is fully consolidated as a subsidiary. Toronto Purchase Trust ($144M settlement) is a separate legal entity excluded from consolidation but distributes annual income to the First Nation. Community Trust Land Claim fund also excluded. Ottawa Trust Fund included on balance sheet with offsetting accumulated surplus. Remuneration schedules referenced (Schedule 4) but detailed figures not provided in extracted text. Health and social services combined as one segment at $3,271,531 - social services portion not separately broken out. Schedule 2 expenditures by object not fully extracted. Statements are amended and restated - original statements were modified.
